scope: Write lock, serialization of concurrent mutations.

tests.name: withWriteLock: two writes started in the same tick run sequentially
withWriteLock: a write that throws does NOT stall subsequent writes
withWriteLock: an async write holds the lock until it resolves
withWriteLock: many writes complete in submission order
withWriteLock: rejection is surfaced to the caller, not swallowed
write serialization: two POST /taxa fired in the same tick produce distinct ids in submission order
write serialization: a PATCH issued same-tick after a POST sees the POSTed taxon
write serialization: POST /reset runs under the lock (§15) — writes before observe pre-reset, after observe empty state
write serialization: two POST /proposals fired in the same tick produce ordered proposal+change ids
write serialization: two POST /changes/.../accept fired in the same tick are serialized, both reflected in live state
write serialization: two POST /changes/.../accept-cascade fired in the same tick serialize cleanly
write serialization: a parent-add and a follow-up read see the consistent post-write state
withWriteLock: FIFO serialization
two concurrent calls serialize in invocation order
ten concurrent calls preserve FIFO across the chain
withWriteLock: throw/reject recovery
a throwing fn propagates the rejection and releases the slot
an async rejection propagates and releases the slot
withWriteLock: synchronous fn is still serialized
ten concurrent increments produce a final value of exactly 10
a synchronous fn returning a value resolves to that value
an async fn returning a value resolves to that value
withWriteLock: slot held until returned promise settles
does not release on first await — releases when fn's promise settles
withWriteLock: independent async code is not blocked
a no-lock promise resolves while a lock-fn is in flight
